![[Pasted image 20260419184536.png]]

Contents of `challenge.txt`

```

```

Since the challenge is named `Onion`, we already know that it is a multi layer encryption/encoding

The text given to us looks `base64` encoded :-

After 1st round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```

After 2nd round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```

After 3rd round of `base64` decoding:-
```

```

After 4th round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```


After 5th round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```

After 6th round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```

After 7th round of `base64` decoding:-

```

```

After 8th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
Vm0xd1IyRXlVWGxVYmtwT1ZsWndVVlpzV21GWFZteHpZVVZPVmxKdVFsbGFWVll3WVd4YWMxTnFSbHBOUjFJelZrUkdZV1JXUm5KaVJscE9WbXR3U1ZaclpEUlpWazVJVkZod1lWSnRhRzlVVmxaM1lVWmFSMWRzV214U2EzQlpWVzEwYjJGV1NYZFhiR2hWVmxkb1JGcEVSbHBsVjFaSVpFWmthR1ZyU1hwV1JscHZWVEZhUjFkc2JGVldSM001
```

After 9th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
Vm1wR2EyUXlUbkpOVlZwUVZsWmFXVmxzYUVOVlJuQllaVVYwYWxac1NqRlpNR1IzVkRGYWRWRnJiRlpOVmtwSVZrZDRZVk5IVFhwYVJtaG9UVlZ3YUZaR1dsWmxSa3BZVW10b2FWSXdXbGhVVldoRFpERlplV1ZIZEZkaGVrSXpWRlpvVTFaR1dsbFVWR3M5
```

After 10th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
VmpGa2QyTnJNVVpQVlZaWVlsaENVRnBYZUV0alZsSjFZMGR3VDFadVFrbFZNVkpIVkd4YVNHTXpaRmhoTVVwaFZGWlZlRkpYUmtoaVIwWlhUVWhDZDFZeWVHdFdhekIzVFZoU1ZGWllUVGs9
```

After 11th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
VjFkd2NrMUZPVVZYYlhCUFpXeEtjVlJ1Y0dwT1ZuQklVMVJHVGxaSGMzZFhhMUphVFZVeFJXRkhiR0ZXTUhCd1YyeGtWazB3TVhSVFZYTTk=
```

After 12th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
V1dwck1FOUVXbXBPZWxKcVRucGpOVnBIU1RGTlZHc3dXa1JaTVUxRWFHbGFWMHBwV2xkVk0wMXRTVXM9
```

After 13th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
WWprME9EWmpOelJqTnpjNVpHSTFNVGswWkRZMU1EaGlaV0ppWldVM01tSUs=
```

After 14th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
Yjk0ODZjNzRjNzc5ZGI1MTk0ZDY1MDhiZWJiZWU3MmIK
```

After 15th round of `base64` decoding:-

```
b9486c74c779db5194d6508bebbee72b
```

Now this sequence of characters does not look like a base64 encoded string. We go to `crackstation.net` to try and figure out what type of hash this is and if a value in its rainbow table matches.

![[Pasted image 20260419185535.png]]

And we got a match! It is a NTLM hash

flag - `CIT{iloveharrypottersomuchthaticouldreadallthebooksintwodaysmostlikely}`
